h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: I think I mark B. `city value` could fit every row, so maybe that fails the value test, while the exact city name belongs to this one first-row cell. So `Value:` should be the printed city name there; is “fits every row” the reject sign?

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The tutor expected L2's correct bar-line marking and counting definition to satisfy the proof question, so the learner would confidently identify the short line as the proof mark.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: LABEL goes on the 3-to-tiles line and the 4-to-quarter-note-card line. BAR-PROOF goes on the bar line after the third quarter-note beat. The number-lines label count and beat unit; the bar line shows this bar is full.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 enacts the new sorting criterion by correctly placing LABEL and BAR-PROOF tags, distinguishing labeling functions from the bar line's proof role.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: places a cover strip over the 3/4 and sets two tags beside the worksheet: LABEL and BAR-PROOF… Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. The short lines no longer settle proof; they label what the numbers mean. With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 stock-takes by declaring the short lines 'no longer settle proof' and introduces a new public mechanism—covering the time signature and deploying LABEL/BAR-PROOF tags to shift the task from drawing lines to sorting functional categories for a rehearsal reader.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: L4 enacts the new sorting criterion by correctly placing LABEL and BAR-PROOF tags, distinguishing labeling functions from the bar line's proof role.

scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: With the head covered, the source is the table. Force of table on cart. The noun fits, so the support card goes there.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner employs the new device (covering the arrowhead) to identify the acting object, shifting from directionality to contact-based labeling.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The frame settled the first card. The arrowhead is still taking over the name. New test: work from what remains. Name the object acting on the cart. Say, “force of ___ on cart.” If the noun fits, place the card.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: Tutor acknowledges that the arrowhead overrides the naming task, then introduces a new device (covering arrowhead) and a changed representation to refocus the learner on the object contact.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: New test: work from what remains. Name the object acting on the cart. Say, “force of ___ on cart.” If the noun fits, place the card.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The covering mechanism precisely and economically removes the directional interference, directly addressing the learner's pressure with a clear, actionable criterion.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no later tutor uptake to assess.
